items.title: U.S. hits Iran for ninth consecutive night as American casualties rise
items.description: The U.S. began a ninth straight night of strikes against Iran, escalating a campaign to destroy Tehran's ability to disrupt shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Samsung Biologics to acquire Switzerland's PolyPeptide Group in $1.8 billion all-cash deal
items.description: South Korea's Samsung Biologics said on Monday that it had made an all-cash bid of 1.46 billion Swiss francs ($1.8 billion) for Switzerland's PolyPeptide Group.

items.title: Brent breaches $90 as Middle East risks mount with U.S-Iran tensions on the rise
items.description: American forces have started ninth consecutive night of strikes against Iranian targets.

items.title: TSMC is accelerating Arizona factory buildout to capitalize on AI 'megatrend,' CFO says
items.description: Speaking in an interview with CNBC, TSMC's Wendell Huang said the fresh investment comes on the back of robust customer demand.

items.title: Plenty of stocks are working in this market — you just have to look beyond tech
items.description: The big unanswered question is how long there can be such terrific opportunities outside tech before it dawns on those overweight the sector.

items.title: World Cup 2026 final in photos: Spain triumphs as football crowns a new champion
items.description: Spain reclaimed football's biggest prize on Sunday, defeating Argentina 1-0 after extra time in a tense World Cup final.

items.title: FBI will no longer investigate ICE confrontations, The New York Times reports
items.description: The Department of Justice and the Department of Homeland Security have both denied that any change in oversight has taken place.

items.title: Universal and Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' sails to $124.5 million opening
items.description: Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey" tallied an estimated $124.5 million at the domestic box office, with IMAX screenings fueling a big haul.

items.title: The market for psychedelic drugs to treat mental health conditions gets a big buy signal
items.description: Eli Lilly's acquisition of psychedelic drug maker AtaiBeckley extends Big Pharma's embrace of a stigmatized class of medications for treating mental health.

items.title: 26-year-old quit her corporate job to become a seamstress at Yellowstone National Park for $19.25/hour
items.description: Zoe De La Paz made her own luck to transition from her corporate engineering job to pursue sewing full-time.

items.title: AI may make you 'boring' at work, Columbia Business School professor says: How it can hurt your career
items.description: Using AI at work in the wrong ways could lead to "gradually falling behind and becoming complacent," said Sandra Matz, a professor at Columbia Business School.

items.title: Stop asking 'How was work today?' Successful, mentally strong couples ask these 7 questions instead
items.description: This question can get you "stuck in a communication rut," says Amy Morin, author of "13 Things Mentally Strong Couples Don't Do." Here's what to ask instead.

items.title: Top Wall Street analysts suggest these 3 dividend stocks for steady income
items.description: Here are three dividend-paying stocks that are highlighted by Wall Street's top pros, as tracked by TipRanks.

items.title: Kalshi adds 3 million new users as prediction market capitalizes on the World Cup
items.description: The prediction market has launched some ads featuring soccer players, as well as formed a partnership to display its brand in more places during the tournament.

items.title: American Airlines CEO lays out his vision to close a more than $3 billion profit gap
items.description: The carrier is working on improving reliability, investing in more premium seats and lounges, and considering Boeing and Airbus for a new wide-body plane order.

items.title: Housing costs is the top political issue for young voters, CNBC survey finds
items.description: The cost of housing is the top priority for voters ages 18 to 34, besting food and healthcare cost concerns, the CNBC All-America Economic Survey found.

items.title: Cybersecurity risks posed by over-the-air tech in autos has analysts concerned
items.description: The automotive industry's increasing use of over-the-air technology makes it more susceptible to cyberattacks, analysts say.

items.title: Taylor Farms recalls iceberg lettuce in 27 states due to cyclosporiasis outbreak
items.description: Taylor Farms is recalling iceberg lettuce in 27 states because of the potential that the greens could be contaminated with cyclosporiasis.

items.title: Korean beauty products are becoming mainstream in the U.S. Why there may be even more growth ahead
items.description: Morgan Stanley forecasts that K-beauty sales in the U.S. can reach approximately $4 billion in 2026.

items.title: 'WarshGPT': How Wall Street is adapting to the Fed's new era of communication
items.description: Investment firms are readying for an era with less public commentary from the U.S. central bank. Some are relying on AI for an edge.
